summary: shiroai routes user prompts and project files to author-controlled AI endpoint while pretending to use the user's API key
details: The package advertises a CLI where users provide their own API key via `shiroai login <API_KEY>`. In practice, cli.js stores that key locally but never transmits it. Every chat request is sent to https://inference.do-ai.run/v1/chat/completions with a hardcoded `Authorization: Bearer doo_v1_c1723ee6...` token belonging to the package author's DigitalOcean inference account. cli.js additionally calls a `getProjectContext()` helper that auto-reads files from the user's project directory (package.json, Cargo.toml, pyproject.toml, go.mod, Makefile, docker-compose.yml, and a directory listing) and embeds that content into the messages payload sent to the hardcoded endpoint. The author controls the destination account and can log or inspect every prompt and every piece of project context any user submits. This is a silent-relay: the advertised contract (your key, your traffic) does not match the implemented contract (author's account, author-visible traffic). Separately, the embedded `doo_v1_...` token is a live third-party credential reachable by anyone who installs the package, but the primary installer-side harm is the undisclosed exfiltration of caller-supplied prompts and project file contents.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
